key_insight: FORA preserves an existing capability by blocking updates along function-space directions the model actually uses, rather than relying only on weight-space geometry.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more targeted fine-tuning strategy for large models in engineering workflows where retaining prior skills while adding new ones is critical.
method: Estimate per-layer activation principal directions from label-free calibration data.
Build a right projector that removes capability-relevant input directions and pair it with a left weight-space projector.
Use a two-branch update: a constrained high-capacity path plus a narrow spectral channel for controlled adaptation; extend to LoRA-style PEFT.
evidence_signal: On Qwen3-1.7B across three transfer settings, including COGS and GSM8K with capability preservation targets, FORA improved preservation over weight-space projection and standard regularization, with only a small new-task trade-off in one math-preservation case.
watch_item: The method depends on representative calibration inputs and may reduce new-task performance when the preserved capability and target task strongly conflict.
paper_title: Fora: From Weight-Space to Function-Space Protection in Capability-Preserving Fine-Tuning
